description:
Letter, probably written by Elazar ha-Kohen b. Shelomo, who worked for(?) the addressee, sending condolences for the death of the dowager and asking for assistance. (Information from Goitein's index cards)

Letter introduced by biblical citations such as Proverbs 3:2 and Isaiah 43:2, from Elʿazar ha-Kohen b. Shelomo ha-Rosh, descendent of geʾonim. Possibly sent to ‘the leader’ Abū l-Mufaḍḍal. Mentions people including Gershom ha-Kohen, al-Ḥaver Ibn Shemaʿya, Yosef, Shelomo, Ibn al-Dabbāgh (son of the tanner) and Ibn al-Qaṭāʾif, and the death of a leader (Rayyis) and a honourable lady (אלגבירה). Contains a blessing for a new child, probably after the death of a son. (Information from CUDL.)

Some of the same phrases appear in T-S NS 226.185 + T-S NS 226.162 (PGPID 24309), another letter by Elʿazar b. Shelomo ha-Kohen.
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
